description: Goal/event compile omits the target population or cohort the contract is about.
Goal/event compile omits the event kind or state transition the contract measures.
Goal/event compile omits which clock defines event time.
Goal/event compile omits the observation window that binds the metric.
Goal/event compile omits the identity of the metric being optimized.
Goal/event compile omits a declared guardrail even though the goal text constrains downside.
Objective and guardrail semantics collapse into the same compiled root role.
An assumption is compiled as if it were an asserted fact.
A limiting constraint is compiled as an objective to maximize rather than a guardrail to respect.
A quantified claim has no explicit domain over which all/exists is interpreted.
A compiled metric refers to a count/rate but the underlying event identity is not declared.
A cross-entity contract needs a join key but compile output omits it.
An aggregate is compiled without a unique subject set, so the counted population is ambiguous.
Compile output omits how unknown/null measurements affect contract truth.
Compile output omits whether absence of evidence means false, unknown, or not-applicable.
Compile output omits the authoritative measurement source for a frontend metric.
Compiled clauses lack trace back to the originating goal/event spans.
Compile narrows or widens meaning but does not mark the loss or approximation.
A single-population single-event contract compiles with explicit population, event, clock, window, and metric identity.
A grouped event-rate contract compiles with domain, grouping key, join key, and unknown/null policy declared.
Objective, guardrail, and assumption compile into distinct typed roles with no collisions.
Frontend compile emits full source-span trace and no-loss markers for an exact semantic compile.
Frontend compile emits trace plus declared widening/narrowing markers for an intentional approximation.
A single-instance measurable leaf lowers exactly to a backend whose capability matrix declares exact support.
A dataset/grouped/windowed leaf lowers exactly to a set-capable backend with explicit grouping and clock binding.
A mixed-backend target composes exact and approximate clauses under an explicit root soundness policy.
Necessity, sufficiency, completeness, assumption-break, and approximation obligations are attached to the relevant claims.
A time-sensitive external flow declares timeout, retry, duplicate, reorder, and staleness runtime obligations.
Latency, cost, authz, and production trace bindings are attached to a production use case.
A WholeSystemTarget closes goal/event source, clause artifacts, exact witness obligation, linked use case, and runtime-quality obligations as one connected trace.
A set-semantics clause omits whether it is universal, existential, threshold, or aggregate.
A set-semantics clause omits the domain over which it quantifies.
An aggregated clause omits its grouping key even though grouping changes the meaning.
A temporal set clause omits which event timestamp drives the window.
One set clause carries conflicting window semantics for the same identity.
A lowered clause names a backend but no machine-readable capability declaration exists for it.
A clause claims exact lowering to a backend that does not support the required semantics.
A clause claims exact lowering but omits the equivalence basis that justifies exactness.
A non-exact lowering omits whether it is sound_over or sound_under.
Canonical IR carries backend-specific syntax instead of backend-neutral semantics.
A single-instance backend is used to represent dataset semantics without an explicit decomposition.
A lowered clause has an artifact identity but no artifact cover relation.
Two different lowered artifacts claim the same artifact identity with incompatible semantics.
A lowered artifact cannot be traced back to the source clause it implements.
A mixed-backend target lacks the root-level policy for composing exact and approximate clauses.
A lowered clause declares a lowering path but omits the backend identity that will carry it.
A lowered clause declares a lowering path but omits whether it is exact or approximate.
A lowered clause declares a lowering path but omits the feature or semantic subset it needs from the backend.
A lowered clause declares a lowering path but omits the artifact identity that should carry the emitted contract.
A time-sensitive external interaction lacks a timeout obligation.
A retried interaction lacks a retry-obligation case.
An event-driven flow lacks a duplicate-event obligation case.
An ordered event flow lacks a reorder-obligation case.
A cached or delayed metric lacks a staleness-obligation case.
A semantic counterexample exists but no replay artifact is bound to it.
A production use case lacks a latency budget.
A production use case lacks a cost budget.
A privileged use case lacks an authorization contract.
A production-near use case lacks a binding to its authoritative trace source.
A necessity claim has no ablation case showing that removing it should break achievement.
A sufficiency claim has no counterexample obligation defining when it should fail.
An OR branch claims completeness but has no gap-scan obligation for omitted alternatives.
An assumption is declared but there is no case describing what breaks when it fails.
A clause depends on an undeclared external dependency or upstream contract.
Two goals trade off against each other but the conflict clause is not declared.
An approximation is allowed but its proof anchor or rationale is missing.
An exact-only clause omits any machine-readable exactness anchor, such as equivalence basis, exactness anchor, or proof target.
A whole-system target claims end-to-end trace closure but omits the goal/event source link.
A whole-system target claims end-to-end trace closure but omits the link to its runtime use case.
A whole-system target lowers an exact clause to an artifact but omits the clause-level witness obligation.
A whole-system target must not achieve when its linked use case still fails a runtime obligation.
A whole-system target claims artifact trace closure but one lowered clause has no artifact identity.
